content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 21, 1990


Robert Novoa
Planning Commissioner
City of Newman
940 Fresno Street
Newman, CA 95360

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. I-90-264 and I-90-282

Dear Mr. Novoa:

You have sought advice regarding your duties and responsibilities as a member of the Newman Planning Commission under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We are unable to provide you with the assistance you seek, for the following reasons.

On March 29, 1990 you were provided telephone advice which concluded that on the basis of the facts presented, there appeared to be a possible conflict of interest with respect to your participation in a forthcoming decision involving a customer of your retail business.² You provided additional information in your letter, dated April 14, 1990.

_________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 This advice was based on application of Section 87103.5 and Regulation 18703.5, copies of which were sent to you. You were also informed that confirmation of the advice, or a further advice letter with respect to your question, could be requested. By letter dated April 4, 1990, you reiterated the factual information pertaining to your retail business and your customers. We could not respond to your letter because it did not seek either formal written advice or confirmation of the prior phone advice. You were informed that if you wished a written response, you needed to submit a request for either confirmation of the telephone advice previously provided to you, or for written advice concerning application of the conflict of interest provisions of the Act to your specific situation.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File Nos. I-90-264 and I-90-282
Page 2


However, it is clear from this letter that there is no specific governmental decision pending before the planning commission, nor was there at the time of your initial telephone inquiries. Instead, the matter about which you originally sought telephone advice concerned a decision that was made by the planning commission at its meeting on February 15, 1990.

Your question, therefore, is simply whether a conflict of interest exists because of your past conduct as a city official. The Commission does not provide advice concerning past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), 18329(c)(4)(A)).

I am sorry that we cannot be of further assistance to you in this matter. If you have any questions please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Does a conflict of interest exist for a Planning Commissioner regarding his participation in a planning commission decision involving a customer of his retail business?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide formal advice because the governmental decision at issue was already made on February 15, 1990, and the FPPC does not provide advice concerning past conduct of city officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. Prior telephone advice was given on March 29, 1990, but formal written advice is declined. Document explains that the matter concerns past conduct (decision made February 15, 1990) rather than a pending decision, and FPPC regulations prohibit advice on past conduct. Document references dual file numbers I-90-264 and I-90-282.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a conflict of interest exist for a Planning Commissioner regarding his participation in a planning commission decision involving a customer of his retail business?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide formal advice because the governmental decision at issue was already made on February 15, 1990, and the FPPC does not provide advice concerning past conduct of city officials.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice to a Newman Planning Commissioner regarding potential conflicts of interest related to a planning commission decision, as the decision had already been made and the FPPC does not advise on past conduct.
